headlineSummary: Microsoft Copilot drew the clearest criticism, while OpenAI and Anthropic were in a close mixed-neutral cluster and Google Gemini had a small positive note on limited evidence [E2][E1][E3].

ranking.summary: Microsoft Copilot was described as deeply disappointing and not delivering the expected AI usefulness, a strongly negative take based on limited evidence [E2].
Anthropic got a mildly negative comparison because Opus initially failed the task before being helped along, and it sits in a close tie with OpenAI [E1].
OpenAI received praise for ChatGPT solving the task on the first try, but that was offset by criticism of its business prospects, leaving it in a close tie with Anthropic [E1][E3].
Google Gemini was mentioned favorably as succeeding on the task, but the signal is based on limited evidence [E1].

evidence.excerpt: Opus(paid) - claimed that "2" is circular. Once I told it that ChatGPT have already done this, finished successfully. ChatGPT(free) - did it from the first try. Grok - produced estimation of the depth of the formula. Gemini - success

annotations.rationale: The commenter reports Claude Opus initially failed the task and only succeeded after being nudged with another model's result, which is a mild negative comparison.
The commenter says ChatGPT completed the task 'from the first try,' which is clear praise in a direct model comparison.
The commenter marks Gemini as a success on the task, which is a favorable evaluation in the comparison.

evidence.excerpt: I honestly don't understand Microsoft's AI strategy. It seems to be built around automating the writing process. If you ask MS 365 Copilot (as opposed to the many other Copilots) what it can do, it's deeply disappointing: "Can you edit the Word document so the format is in line with these requirements?" "No, but I can help you draft an implementation consistent with the requirements." "Can you add this section to the 35 individual copies of this document in this OneDrive folder?" "No, but I can help you draft [something]." This is NOT the AI revolution anyone was waiting for.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly names MS 365 Copilot, calls it 'deeply disappointing,' and says it is 'NOT the AI revolution anyone was waiting for,' which is strong criticism of capability and usefulness.

evidence.excerpt: It now looks like AI is a death march. You must spend billions of dollars to have the best model or you won't be able to sell inference. But even if you do, a whole host of better funded competitors are going to beat you within months so your inference charges better pay off extremely quickly. When the gap between models starts to drop, distribution becomes king and OpenAI can't compete in that field either. Google can do that. Meta can do that. MSFT probably can do that. Amazon can do that. OpenAI cannot. They do not have the cash to do it.

annotations.rationale: The commenter argues OpenAI cannot compete on distribution and lacks the cash to keep up, which is strong criticism of its competitive position and business prospects.
